---
title: "Lab 2 Instructions"
author: "Nicky Wakim"
subtitle: "PUBH 523/623"
date-modified: "today"
format: 
  html:
    link-external-newwindow: true
    toc: true
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 4
    code-annotations: hover
    page-layout: full
    highlight-style: ayu
    code-block-bg: "#f8f9fa"       # Sets the background color
    code-block-border-left: "#5BAFF8" # Enables and colors a thick left border
  pdf: default 
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
execute:
  freeze: auto 
---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| echo: false

# PLEASE DO NOT REMOVE THIS CODE CHUNK!!!
### ADD YOUR LIBRARIES HERE!!! ####
pacman::p_load( # These are my goto packages to load
  here, 
  tidyverse, 
  writexl, 
  rstatix, 
  janitor, 
  naniar,
  gt,
  gtsummary,
  readxl
)
```

```{=html}
<style>
/* Hide numbers for H1 and H2 in the main body */
h1 .header-section-number, 
h2 .header-section-number {
    display: none;
}
</style>
```

## Directions {.unnumbered}

[You can download the `.qmd` file for this lab here.](https://github.com/nwakim/PUBH_523_26Su/blob/main/project/LastName_FirstInit_Lab_01.qmd)

The above link will take you to your **editing** file. **Please do not remove anything from this editing file!!** You will only add your code and work to this file.

## Purpose {.unnumbered}

**The purpose of this lab is to prepare our data for further investigation using several data transformations.** We will filter to our target subpopulation, select only the variables relevant to our project, and create new variables that combine information from multiple source columns. **This is meant to take your practice on data transformation one step further: translating written human language to the needed action, function, and arguments.**

## Lab activities

+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Project                                                                   | Population                                                                                                                                           | Variables                                                                                                          | Composite score                                                            |

| How does food support systems help individuals                            | People under 100% FPL (filter for) and people who received free groceries or meals in the past 12 months (consequence of answering Q26D)             | FOOD_INSEC,                                                                                                        | Composite score of ease of access to free groceries or meals (Q6D's added) |
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                    |                                                                            |
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      | Q26D (1-4), up to 3 other variables that you're interested in!                                                     |                                                                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| How does trust and fair treatment at doctors effect self-reported health? | People who identify as LGBTQ                                                                                                                         | Q108B_2 (trust), Q70F_4, Q70F_5 (trt or judged unfairly bc of gender ID or sex oreint), Q27 (self-reported health) | Trust, not judged, and treated fairly (1 add for Q108B_2, Q70F_4, Q70F_5)  |
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                    |                                                                            |
|                                                                           | (You can change this population if you want. For example, you may want to focus on anti-fat bias at the doctor's office. Check out Q70F for options) |                                                                                                                    |                                                                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| How does benefits received effect mental health of parents?               | People who have at least one child in the household                                                                                                  | Measures of mental health (Q30s),                                                                                  | Total mental health, total beenfits                                        |
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                    |                                                                            |
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      | Measures of benefits (Q53s)                                                                                        |                                                                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                    |                                                                            |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+

### Filtering to Your Target Subpopulation

To implement an asset-focused analysis, we must first isolate the subpopulation relevant to your scope using `filter()`.

- *If Option 1 or 3 (Food/Housing Safety Net):* Filter your data to households navigating budget constraints, defined as those living below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (`FPL_LT50 == 1 | FPL50_100 == 1 | FPL100_150 == 1 | FPL150_200 == 1`).
- *If Option 2 (Healthcare Access):* Filter to individuals relying on public coverage options.
- *If Option 4 (Work Support):* Filter to active working families or households with children (`NUM_CHILD_HH >= 1`).

::: callout-important
#### Task

Write a pipeline that filters the dataset down to your specific population of interest. Save this as a new object called `wbns_project_sub`. Print out the number of rows before and after filtering to verify the operation.
:::

### Selecting Project Variables

Your dataset contains hundreds of variables. For project clarity, reduce your data frame to only columns essential for your scope.

::: callout-important
#### Task

Use `select()` to keep only the following columns in your subset data frame: `ID`, your demographic grouping variables (e.g., `PPETHM`, `PPGENDER`, or `PPAGE`), your programmatic support variables, and your outcome variable (e.g., `Q23`).
:::

### Rename variables for clarity

### Constructing the Combined Asset Index

Your project must create a brand-new variable that combines information from at least two source columns using `mutate()`. For example, in Option 1, you can add up program binary metrics to calculate a total safety net index.

Use `if_else()` or `case_when()` inside your mutate pipeline to catch and handle missing values or refusals, converting them to `0` or `NA` so they do not distort your math.

::: callout-important
#### Task

Create your new combined asset metric using `mutate()`. Write a brief paragraph explaining:

1.  Which source variables you chose.
2.  The code logic you applied to handle missing values or refusals.
3.  What a high score vs. low score means on your new metric.
:::

## Remove NA's

### Inverting a Deficit Variable into an Asset Lens

Many survey questions are framed around deficits (e.g., `Q23` asks about eating less due to lack of money). To fulfill our asset-based goal, we will use `case_when()` to invert this into a positive framing. For example, a response of "No" to `Q23` means a family maintained full dietary stability.

::: callout-important
#### Task

Use `mutate()` and `case_when()` to transform your primary health/well-being outcome variable into an asset-framed factor variable. Use `glimpse()` to display your final, transformed data frame.
:::
